Event Name: 22nd Annual Kappa League Scholarship and Awards Showcase
Event Description: Get ready for the 22nd Annual Kappa League Scholarship and Awards Showcase - a night of celebrating achievements and supporting education! Kappa League is not just an organization; it's a movement dedicated to empowering young men across the valley. Through mentorship, career opportunities, and exposure, Kappa League instills values of leadership, personal growth, and academic excellence in its members. Our program brings together successful men with 6th – 12th grade male students from diverse backgrounds, nurturing their potential and guiding them towards a brighter future. The Annual Kappa League Showcase is the end-of-year celebration highlighting the accomplishments of the Kappa League Scholars. It serves as an opportunity for the Scholars to showcase skills they've learned during the program. It's also an opportunity to raise funds for the Kappa League program to ensure the programs continued success Da’Shaun Holmes, a proud native of St. Petersburg, FL, is a shining example of what focus, drive, and a passion for learning can accomplish. At The Canterbury School of Florida, he distinguished himself early—excelling academically while actively contributing to organizations such as Mu Alpha Theta and the Science National Honor Society. With a clear vision for his future, Da’Shaun pursued Mechanical Engineering at Florida Atlantic University, where his dedication and ingenuity quickly set him apart. In December 2023, he earned his bachelor’s degree and made history at FAU’s Senior Design Showcase, becoming the first student to win both the People’s Choice Award and the Judge’s Choice Award for his innovative senior project. Before crossing the graduation stage, Da’Shaun was selected for a competitive internship with Honeywell Aerospace in Phoenix, AZ, where he trained as an Advanced Manufacturing Engineer. His performance led to a full-time offer in his hometown—but his ambition didn’t stop there. Recognizing where he could make the greatest impact, Da’Shaun returned to Arizona, earning a promotion and continuing to excel in his engineering career. Today, Da’Shaun not only stands as a rising leader in aerospace engineering but also as a proud member of Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc., embracing excellence, achievement, and service in everything he pursues

Event Name: Tarot Curd: A Cheese & Card Reading
Event Description: An unusual pairing or a divine match? Tarot and cheese have more in common than you think! Ever wonder what your cheese says about you? (Us neither, until now.) Join us for an evening where ancient mysticism meets artisan dairy - because why shouldn't cheese have its fortune told? We're pairing six spectacular American cheeses with Major Arcana tarot cards, exploring the stories, personalities, and straight-up magic in each wheel. Pull a card at the door. Find your cheese on the board. Taste your way through fresh chèvre innocence, cave-aged wisdom, and the beautiful chaos of blue mold breaking through. We'll talk about transformation, partnership, disruption, and joy - all through the lens of really, really good cheese. No tarot experience needed. No cheese expertise required. Just bring your curiosity and maybe a sense of humor about the whole thing. This is mysticism you can eat. Fortune-telling you can taste. The Whey of the Cards. Space is limited to keep things intimate. Grab your spot before they vanish into the ether (or, you know, sell out).

Event Name: Couples Workshop-Hold Me Tight®
Event Description: Couples will learn to overcome negative stuck spots, communicate for closeness and care, and heal relational hurts and injuries. Our Hold Me Tight® Workshop for Couples is based on Attachment Theory, Emotionally Focused Couples Therapy, and the international best-selling book "Hold Me Tight: Seven Conversations for a Lifetime of Love" by Dr. Sue Johnson. In our workshop, you will learn what is at the root of most conflicts between you and your partner, and how to change those moments through a proven method that all couples can do. Investing in a couples workshop as a new couple, engaged or newlywed pair is one of the most proactive and rewarding steps you can take for your relationship. These workshops equip you with tools to navigate challenges, communicate effectively, and build a strong foundation rooted in mutual understanding and respect. Think of it as preventative care for your love—addressing potential issues before they arise and fostering a deeper connection that lasts. Prioritize this relationship early!**This is also a great gift to consider for loved ones and family members who are wanting to learn about creating healthy relationship foundations but might not have the resources to attend** For couples who have been together for years, a couples workshop can reignite the spark and strengthen the connection that first brought you together. Over time, life’s demands can lead to communication gaps or unspoken frustrations, but this workshop provides a safe and supportive space to reconnect. You’ll learn new strategies to deepen intimacy, resolve conflicts, and rediscover the joy in your partnership. Whether you’re navigating a rough patch or simply seeking to grow together, this experience can breathe new life into your relationship, fostering a bond that’s resilient and more fulfilling than ever. You will not be doing large group sharing as much as having private conversations with your partner and the two of you have an option to utilize the therapists that are assisting to help with these conversations. This workshop will be facilitated by Michelle Hildt and the Therapy With Heart Team. Hold Me Tight® is a registered trademark to Sue Johnson

Event Name: Ancestral Artistry-2026 Arizona Architectural Film Showcase
Event Description: The film explores the legacy of Black craftsmen and builders whose artistry shaped Louisiana and New Orleans unique history and culture. Produced by the Louisiana Architecture Foundation, this film explores the legacy of Black craftsmen and builders whose artistry shaped Louisiana’s architectural identity. The film explores a centuries-old legacy rooted in craftsmanship, culture, and resilience—a story that begins over 300 years ago with the arrival of enslaved Africans in French colonial Louisiana. These individuals brought with them not only strength and endurance, but deep knowledge in building trades like carpentry, masonry, iron smithing, and plasterwork. Their skill shaped the physical and cultural landscape of Louisiana in profound and lasting ways. Over generations, these trades were passed down through families and communities, evolving into a distinctive architectural identity that sets Louisiana apart from other parts of the United States. These traditions took root in a place unlike much of America—a colony and later state that fostered a significant population of free people of color, many of mixed African and European descent. For these Creoles of Color, the building trades were more than a means of survival; they became a path to wealth, independence, and social mobility in an era when African-descended people faced severe restrictions across much of the country. The film illuminates how African and European influences merged in Louisiana's architecture through both technique and artistry. From finely crafted ironwork and ornate plaster details to expertly constructed homes and public buildings, the contributions of these Black and Creole artisans helped define Louisiana’s unique built environment. Yet their names and stories are often absent from history books, their contributions underrecognized and at risk of being forgotten. Butler Housing Company Coldwell Banker Historic Homes Group
Place: Third Street Theater, Phoenix Center for the Arts

Join special guest, Gabriel Logan Braun, world musician, loop artist & teacher of somatic healing arts, for this one time workshop experience where he weaves together Qi Gong, Primal Movement, Breathwork & Sound Healing. There is so much that we can’t control and that is unknown to us in our current world situation. What we can do is connect to our passion for life and the flame of who we are. You are invited to come together to light your inner fire, burn through stagnation & send out your powerful intentions! We will begin the evening cultivating our energy through the ancient healing art of Qi Gong. As we build our inner fire, we will move into primal movement, connecting to the wild and untamed parts of our nature. Be prepared to move, dance and shake to live world music! This will be followed by Wim Hof-inspired breathwork to release, let go & clear our system of accumulated stress and tension. We will complete with increased vitality reduced stress inner calm mental clarity a balanced nervous system restored energy having fun! Opening circle / Intentions 45 mins – Primal Fire Practice – primal movement, qi gong, yoga, dance & breathwork to both recorded and live music 1hr – Sonic Womb Live – sound healing journey / sound bath 15 mins - Closing integration circle Growing up in Los Angeles, California, Gabriel was exposed to many different world music influences from an early age. When he was sixteen, he went through a dark night of the soul. After being guided to his first yoga class, he began practicing yoga & meditation, traveling the world, studying with healers and musical mentors and has been on a path of personal growth and spiritual development ever since. All of these experiences led him to create his own unique path of sound healing and music that he is here to share with the world. For the past 30 + years, he has been involved in various forms of both inner and outer movement practices including gymnastics, martial arts, dance, yoga, tai chi, qi gong, primal movement and capoeira.

Event Name: Evicshen at Linger Longer Lounge
Event Description: Experimental musician Evicshen performing live for Desert Drone at Linger Longer Lounge. Victoria Shen is an experimental music performer and sound artist from San Francisco. Though analog synthesizers are the backbone of Shen’s music, while performing live, she plays self-made electronics, invented instruments, and even acoustic objects like a bull whip. Shen is a markedly mobile performer that muddles the boundaries between performer and audience, stage and floor. Her sets tend to be site-specific, in direct response to the physical and acoustic qualities of the space in which she performs. Her live show vacillates between moments of restraint and swells of frenetic and confrontational movement. A tightrope walk between control and chance, the whiff of danger is never far during Shen’s performances. Her sound is dynamic with a sensitivity to texture and structure throughout. with local support from Lauren Sarah Hayes. Doors 7:30pm Music 8:00pm

Event Name: Self-Care City Scavenger Hunt: Based on Hot Habits Series - Phoenix Area
Event Description: Self-Care City Scavenger Hunt – Based on Hot Habits Book Series. A fun hunt in your city to help you explore the sites and build wellness! Revitalize your approach to personal development with our "Self-Care City Scavenger Hunt!" This event harnesses the dynamic VARK (Visual, Auditory, Read/Write, Kinesthetic) learning methodologies to create an engaging, self-guided adventure. Perfect for individuals or groups, it offers a fun and interactive way to explore wellness, which encompasses seven critical areas: Physical, Emotional, Intellectual, Social, Spiritual, Environmental, and Occupational. Our scavenger hunt is designed to support these facets of wellness through targeted self-care habits inspired by our Hot Habits series. Dive into this transformative experience to engage all your senses, explore your city, and create memorable moments that promote personal growth and lasting change. Each aspect of the event is crafted to support habit formation in a way that resonates best with your personal learning preferences, ensuring a fulfilling and transformative experience. Available as an add-on to your Self-Care Scavenger Hunt experience. These guides are your essential companions for building a sustainable habit offering practical, research-backed strategies to help you integrate effective reading seamlessly into your daily routine. Embark on a transformative journey to form your new habit in 90 days, following the 21/90 rule: it takes 21 days to build a habit and 90 days to make it a lifestyle. The guide includes access to an app with daily prompts and tracking features.

Event Name: Introduction to Kumiko
Event Description: Introduction to Kumiko Join us for an inside look of Kumiko the Japanese art form of building decorative screens. In this class we’ll dive into the history of Kumiko and its origins. Look at the various styles, tools and jigs used by the masters themselves all while building two decorative 5”x 5” screen tiles in both Asanoha and Izutsu style.

Event Name: Make a Cutting Board
Event Description: Make a cherry cutting board with handle & juice groove! Beginner-friendly. All tools & materials included. In this hands-on woodworking class, you’ll make your own hardwood cutting board complete with a handle cutout and juice groove. Using beautiful domestic cherry wood and a variety of Rockler tools and jigs, you’ll learn fundamental woodworking techniques to create a durable and functional piece that looks great in any kitchen. The finished board will measure approximately ¾" x 12" x 16". What You’ll Do: • Learn to glue up a cutting board blank using Rockler’s Mini Deluxe Panel Clamps • Experience Rockler’s unique “Pay-It-Forward” process — the board you glue will be used in a future class, and you’ll receive a pre-glued board to continue your project • Flatten your cutting board using a drum sander • Square up the board using a table saw • Route a handle cutout using Rockler’s 4-in-1 Cutting Board Handle Routing Template and a compact router • Add a juice groove with Rockler’s Juice Groove Jig • Sand all edges, corners, and surfaces until smooth • Apply a food-safe finish (Odie’s Oil) • Install non-slip rubber feet to complete your board What’s Included: • All materials, domestic cherry hardwood, and supplies • Use of Rockler tools, jigs, and equipment • Rockler safety gear (provided at no additional charge) Important Info: • Class is beginner-friendly — no prior woodworking experience required • All safety gear and materials are included
